name: Apache Pulsar Python Client

auth.notes: JWT tokens, TLS client certificates, OAuth2 (Astra Streaming)

performance.notes: C++ extension backend; low latency pub/sub; multi-million msg/sec throughput

agent_readiness.notes: Python client for Apache Pulsar messaging. import pulsar. client = pulsar.Client('pulsar://localhost:6650'). producer = client.create_producer('my-topic'). producer.send('hello'.encode()). consumer = client.subscribe('my-topic', 'my-sub'). msg = consumer.receive(). consumer.acknowledge(msg). Pulsar's differentiators vs Kafka: multi-tenancy built-in, geo-replication, tiered storage, schema registry. Subscriptions: Exclusive, Shared, Failover, Key_Shared. Async client support.

gotchas: Smaller ecosystem than Kafka (fewer connectors and integrations)
More complex cluster setup than RabbitMQ
Schema evolution requires Pulsar schema registry setup
best_when: Tiered storage for unlimited message retention
When Kafka's operational complexity is too high
avoid_when: When Kafka ecosystem (Flink, Kafka Connect) is required
Simple task queues (RabbitMQ or Redis Streams simpler)
summary: Apache Pulsar Python client for pub/sub messaging. pulsar.Client('pulsar://...').create_producer('topic').send(msg). Consumer subscriptions: Exclusive, Shared, Failover. Built-in multi-tenancy, geo-replication, tiered storage (vs Kafka add-ons). C++ extension backend for performance. Smaller ecosystem than Kafka but cleaner architecture. DataStax Astra Streaming for managed Pulsar.
